title: Two-stage locking catheter splice assembly
abstract: Various embodiments include a locking catheter splice assembly that may include a multi-stage barb fitting, a first locking member, and a second locking member. The multi-stage barb fitting may have a first end including a first set of barbs and a second end including a second set of barbs. The first end may be configured to be inserted into the first catheter and the second end may be configured to be inserted into the second catheter. The first locking member may be slipped over the first catheter so as to surround the portion of the first end of the multi-stage barb inserted into the first catheter. The second locking member may be slipped over the second catheter so as to surround the portion of the second end of the multi-stage barb inserted into the second catheter. The first and second locking members may interlock with one another when pressed together.
claims: Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed is: 1. A locking catheter splice assembly for joining a first catheter and a second catheter, comprising: a multi-stage barb fitting having a first end including a first set of barbs and a second end including a second set of barbs, wherein the first end is configured to be inserted into the first catheter and the second end is configured to be inserted into the second catheter; a first locking member configured to be slipped over the first catheter and to surround a portion of the first end of the multi-stage barb fitting inserted into the first catheter; and a second locking member configured to be slipped over the second catheter and to surround the portion of the second end of the multi-stage barb fitting inserted into the second catheter, wherein the first and second locking members are configured to interlock with one another when pressed together. 2. The locking catheter splice assembly of claim 1, wherein the first locking member is configured to compress an outer diameter of a portion of the first catheter against at least one of the first set of barbs when the first and second locking members are interlocked with one another. 3. The locking catheter splice assembly of claim 1, wherein the first locking member includes an inner cavity having a first catheter trapping surface separate from a second catheter trapping surface, wherein the first and second catheter trapping surfaces are axially aligned with a first barb and a second barb when the first locking member and the second locking member are interlocked with one another. 4. The locking catheter splice assembly of claim 1, wherein each of the first and second sets of barbs include a first barb and a second barb, wherein the first barb has a smaller outermost diameter than the second barb. 5. The locking catheter splice assembly of claim 1, wherein the multi-stage barb fitting is symmetrical about a longitudinal axis. 6. The locking catheter splice assembly of claim 1, wherein one of the first and second locking members includes a latching tab configured to be received in a latching slot included in the other of the first and second locking members for interlocking the first and second locking members. 7. The locking catheter splice assembly of claim 6, wherein the latching slot extends radially through the other of the first and second locking members that includes the latching slot. 8. The locking catheter splice assembly of claim 6, wherein the other of the first and second locking members that includes the latching slot further includes a guide slot configured to guide movement of the first and second locking members toward one another along a longitudinal axis of the multi-stage barb fitting. 9. The locking catheter splice assembly of claim 1, wherein the multi-stage barb fitting includes a central portion positioned between the first and second ends that is wider than the first and second sets of barbs. 10. The locking catheter splice assembly of claim 9, wherein the central portion includes a gripping surface configured to facilitate gripping of the central portion during surgery. 11. The locking catheter splice assembly of claim 9, wherein a gap is formed between a largest outer diameter of the central portion and a largest inner diameter of at least one of the first and second locking members when the first and second locking members are mated together to couple the first and second catheters using the multi stage barb fitting. 12. A method for joining a first catheter and a second catheter, comprising: slipping a first locking member over the first catheter; slipping a second locking member over the second catheter; inserting a multi-stage barb fitting into open ends of both the first and second catheters, wherein a first end of the multi-stage barb fitting includes a first set of barbs and a second end of the multi-stage barb fitting includes a second set of barbs; and pressing the first and second locking members together over the multi-stage barb fitting until the first and second locking members interlock.
